mnemonics.title: TM Components - Q S G B F
mnemonics.content: Q Σ Γ δ q0 B F - 'Queens Start Going Before Final' - Remember: Q (States), S (Sigma=Input alphabet), G (Gamma=Tape alphabet), B (Blank), F (Final states)

mnemonics.title: Church-Turing Memory Trick
mnemonics.content: C = Computable - Church said TM can compute anything 'C'omputable. T = Turing formalized it. The 'C' connects Church to Computable.

mnemonics.title: Transition Function δ
mnemonics.content: δ: 'Drink' Q with Gamma to get new Q, Gamma, and direction - δ(q, X) = (p, Y, D) - p=next state, Y=write symbol, D=direction

acronyms.usage: Theoretical computing device with infinite tape that can simulate any algorithm
TM where each configuration yields at most one successor configuration
TM where each configuration can yield multiple possible successors
A TM that can simulate any other TM - the concept behind stored-program computers
Language class accepted by TM - can enumerate all strings in the language
Less powerful than TM - can be simulated by TM but cannot simulate TM
Set of input symbols that can appear in the input string (excludes blank)
Set of all symbols that can be written on tape = Σ ∪ {B}

visualTips.visualization: Imagine a long paper tape (infinite) with a box (read/write head) that slides left/right. The box has a light that reads the symbol and can print a new symbol. Inside the box is a 'brain' (finite control) with states.
Draw a flowchart: Start → Process. If enter final state → ACCEPT (stop). If no transition defined → REJECT (stop). If no final state reached but transitions continue → LOOP forever.
Use concentric circles: Inner circle = Regular (smallest), Middle = Context-Free, Outer = Recursive (decidable), Largest = RE (recognizable). Recursive ⊂ RE.
Imagine a 'halting detector' machine H. Feed H to itself - creates paradox. If H says 'halts', it loops; if H says 'loops', it halts. Contradiction proves undecidable.

analogies.analogy: Like an infinitely long conveyor belt in a factory - there's always more space to work with, never runs out of room for intermediate results.
Like a smartphone - just as a smartphone can run any app (simulate any program), a UTM can run any algorithm (simulate any TM).
Like the metric system for measurement - just as metric provides standard units, TM provides standard definition for 'computation'.
Like the 'mode' button on a microwave - different states (popcorn, defrost, reheat) determine how the machine reacts to the same input.
Like a game character's action table - if in state 'idle' and see 'enemy', go to 'attack' state and perform 'sword swing'.
Decidable = Judge who always gives verdict (guilty/not guilty). Recognizable = Detective who finds evidence for guilt but may never conclude innocence.
